equipment.description: Large vertical pump used to draw seawater into the desalination facility.
Rapid mixer used to evenly distribute coagulants in the raw water.
Slow mixer to promote the agglomeration of particles into larger flocs.
Tank using microbubbles to float and remove suspended solids and oils.
Basin or tank containing sand media to remove suspended solids by gravity filtration.
Closed vessel containing sand media, operating under pressure for higher filtration rates.
Vessel housing multiple filter cartridges for fine particulate removal before RO membranes.
Pre-assembled unit containing UF membrane modules to remove fine particles and pathogens.
Multi-stage centrifugal pump providing the high pressure required for reverse osmosis.
Device transferring pressure from the high-pressure brine reject to the low-pressure feed water.
Turbine driven by the high-pressure brine reject to assist the high-pressure RO feed pump.
Fiberglass reinforced plastic tubular vessel housing reverse osmosis membrane elements.
Pump transferring the concentrated brine reject to the outfall or disposal system.
Pump moving permeate (product water) to post-treatment or storage.
Vessel containing limestone to add calcium and alkalinity to the RO permeate.
System injecting carbon dioxide into permeate to dissolve limestone for remineralization.
Small positive displacement pump dosing sodium hypochlorite for disinfection.
Pump injecting chemicals to prevent scale formation on RO membranes.
Pump dosing sodium bisulfite to neutralize free chlorine before RO membranes.
Tank made of compatible material storing treatment chemicals.
Automated screen removing large debris from incoming wastewater.
Screen with small openings for removing finer debris and plastics.
Chamber using a vortex flow to settle out heavy inorganic grit.
Equipment used to wash and dewater separated grit.
Large basin allowing suspended solids to settle as primary sludge.
Mechanism scraping settled sludge to a hopper in rectangular clarifiers.
Rotating bridge with scraper blades pushing sludge to a central hopper in circular clarifiers.
High-speed centrifugal blower providing air for biological treatment.
Lobe-type blower supplying constant volume air for smaller aeration tanks.
Porous disk or tube releasing fine air bubbles into the mixed liquor.
Pump returning settled biological sludge from secondary clarifiers to the aeration basin.
Pump removing excess biological sludge from the system.
Channel containing ultraviolet lamps to deactivate pathogens in the effluent.
Equipment generating ozone gas for advanced oxidation or disinfection.
Baffled basin providing contact time for ozone to react with water.
Tank designed to concentrate sludge by gravity settling.
Tank thickening light waste activated sludge using dissolved air.
Large heated and mixed tank for biological breakdown of sludge, producing biogas.
System using compressed biogas injected into draft tubes to mix digester contents.
Compressor handling biogas for mixing or transfer to cogeneration units.
High-speed decanter centrifuge separating water from digested sludge.
Machine squeezing water from sludge between two porous belts.
System preparing and injecting polymer solution to condition sludge for dewatering.
Pump suitable for transferring viscous, high-solids sludge.
Automated system for circulating cleaning chemicals through membrane systems.
Online instrument measuring the cloudiness or haziness of water.
Instrument continuously monitoring the acidity or alkalinity of the water.
Instrument measuring water conductivity to estimate total dissolved solids (TDS).
Instrument measuring the amount of active chlorine remaining in the water.
Instrument monitoring pressure drop across filters or membranes to indicate fouling.
Assembly controlling electrical power to multiple motors in the plant.
Drive used to control the speed of large pumps to match varying flow demands.
